text:
` 
Work Report 2024-25 
School Ayush Herbal Garden Report 
Organization Name : Maharshi Dayanand Jan Kalyan Sanstha, Bhopal 
Project Proposal : Devlopment of School Ayush herbal Garden in Madhya Pradesh. 
Project No. : Z-18017/188/CSS/NGO/SHG/MP-02/2023-24-NMPB-VIII  
No. Of School : 25 
Introduction: 
AYUSH School Herbal Garden is a wonderful initiative aimed at making students aware about the 
medicinal benefits of herbs and plants. Under this project, herbal plants have been planted in selected 25 
schools of Bhopal. Along with this, display plates and banners have also been installed to provide 
information about each plant. This effort provides a unique opportunity to the students to understand the 
natural remedies and importance of plants. 
Objectives: 
1 To plant herbal plants in 25 different schools and display information about them. 
2 To provide information about each plant through display plates and banners. 
3 Educating students and the community about the medicinal properties of herbal plants and their uses. 
Action Details: 
1 Selection and Planting of Plants: Herbal plants have been planted in 25 selected schools of Bhopal. 
These plants have medicinal properties and are known to benefit health. These plants include Tulsi, 
Amla, Aloe Vera, Adusa, Neem, Sadabahar, Nirgundi, Ashwagandha, Kapoor Tulsi and many other 
plants. These plants have been planted in the garden of each school, giving students a chance to connect 
with nature and learn about plants. 
2 Display Plates and Banners for Information: A display plate or banner has been placed near each 
plant, which gives detailed information about the name, use and medicinal properties of that plant. These 
banners help students understand the importance of plants and also inspire them to use medicinal plants 
correctly. 
3 Content of banners and display plates: Each banner and display plate contains the scientific name of 
the plant, its number, medicinal properties, use, and cultural information. For example, the banner for 
Tulsi contains its name and medicinal value. Apart from this, practical information about the uses of each 
plant is also given to the students, such as the use of aloe vera and its effect on the skin. 
4 Protection of plants: In some schools, bamboo or iron nets have been installed to protect each plant, 
so that each medicinal plant can be protected from small children.

` 
5 Number of medicinal plants: 20 or more species of medicinal plants have been planted in each 
school. The number of medicinal plants in each species has been determined on the basis of the 
characteristics and requirement of that species so that proper care and growth of every species is 
possible." 
Benefits and Objectives: 
Education and Awareness: The main objective of this initiative is to spread awareness about medicinal 
plants among students. By getting information about these plants, students will be able to understand the 
importance of natural treatment methods. 
Health Benefits: Medicinal properties of herbal plants will help in improving the health of students. This 
initiative will help students understand the importance of natural treatment for their health. 
Environmental Protection: Establishment of herbal garden will also make the attitude of students 
positive towards the environment. By understanding the importance of plants, students can take steps for 
environmental protection. 
Conclusion: 
The Ayush School Herbal Garden Project is an innovative initiative aimed at educating students about the 
medicinal benefits of herbal plants and providing them an opportunity to connect with nature. Through 
the herbal plants and information banners installed in selected 25 schools of Bhopal, this project not only 
made students aware about the importance of plants but also inspired them to adopt a natural lifestyle. 
This initiative is not only important from the educational point of view but it is also a big step towards 
health and environmental protection.
